hero.headline: O.P. Jindal Global University 1-Year Online MBA in Human Resource Management: Fees, Syllabus and Career Scope
hero.sub: India's only 1-year fully online MBA. AACSB accredited. Institution of Eminence (IoE). 72 credits in 12 months. QS 5 Star rated.

sections.tldr: JGU's 1-Year Online MBA in Human Resource Management covers Performance Management, Talent Management, Compensation and Benefits, and Employee Wellbeing across 72 credits compressed into 12 months. AACSB accredited (held by ~6% of B-schools globally) and backed by IoE status from the Ministry of Education. Built for HR professionals with 3-8 years of experience in talent acquisition, HR business partnering, L&D, or total rewards who want an internationally benchmarked MBA in one year.

about.body:
O.P. Jindal Global University (JGU) is one of only four private universities in India to hold Institution of Eminence (IoE) status, declared by the Ministry of Education under UGC (Institutions of Eminence Deemed to be Universities) Regulations, 2017. This IoE designation grants JGU the autonomy to design innovative programmes, including India's only 1-year fully online MBA.

The Human Resource Management specialisation pairs a rigorous management core (Strategic Management, Financial Management, Business Analytics, Marketing, Operations, Leadership) with four HR elective subjects: Performance Management, Talent Management, Compensation and Benefits, and Employee Wellbeing. The programme runs 72 credits in 12 months, the same credit load as conventional 2-year MBA programmes compressed into an accelerated format.

JGU holds AACSB accreditation, a global quality standard met by only about 6% of business schools worldwide and roughly 1% in India. The university is also QS 5 Star rated and UGC recognised. The HRM MBA targets HR professionals in talent management, HR business partnering, organisational development, or total rewards who need a management degree that matches their professional ambitions without a two-year pause. Full programme details at /universities/op-jindal-global-university-online/mba.

subjects.name: The Firm and the Consumer
subjects.description: Analyse microeconomic foundations of firm behaviour, consumer demand theory, pricing strategy, and market structures relevant to managerial decision-making.

subjects.name: Global Economics
subjects.description: Examine macroeconomic indicators, international trade theory, exchange rate dynamics, and fiscal and monetary policy impacts on global business operations.

subjects.name: Business Statistics
subjects.description: Apply probability distributions, hypothesis testing, regression analysis, and statistical inference to business data for evidence-based decision-making.

subjects.name: Financial Management
subjects.description: Cover capital budgeting, cost of capital estimation, working capital management, dividend policy, and corporate financing decisions.

subjects.name: Marketing Management
subjects.description: Study market segmentation, product positioning, pricing frameworks, distribution channel design, and integrated marketing communication strategies.

subjects.name: Organisational Behaviour
subjects.description: Explore individual motivation theories, group dynamics, leadership models, organisational culture, and change management approaches in modern workplaces.

subjects.name: Operations Management
subjects.description: Learn process design, capacity planning, inventory control, quality management, and lean operations principles for service and manufacturing contexts.

subjects.name: Business Analytics
subjects.description: Apply data visualisation, predictive modelling, and prescriptive analytics tools to transform business data into actionable management insights.

subjects.name: Strategic Management
subjects.description: Develop competitive strategy using industry analysis, resource-based views, diversification frameworks, and strategic execution plans for organisations.

subjects.name: Leadership
subjects.description: Build leadership capabilities through self-awareness frameworks, influence strategies, team management practices, and ethical leadership in organisational contexts.

subjects.name: Performance Management
subjects.description: Design performance appraisal systems, set OKR and KPI frameworks, implement continuous feedback mechanisms, and link performance outcomes to organisational strategy.

subjects.name: Talent Management
subjects.description: Build end-to-end talent pipelines covering workforce planning, employer branding, succession planning, high-potential identification, and retention strategy.

subjects.name: Compensation and Benefits
subjects.description: Structure total rewards programmes including base pay, variable pay, equity compensation, benefits design, and pay equity audits aligned with market benchmarking.

subjects.name: Employee Wellbeing
subjects.description: Design end-to-end employee wellbeing programmes covering mental health support, work-life integration, burnout prevention, and organisational resilience frameworks.

syllabus.note: JGU's 1-year MBA covers 72 credits in 12 months. Core subjects span the first two trimesters. Specialisation electives are chosen in the final trimester alongside a capstone project.

whoHires.body:
HRM MBA graduates from JGU's 1-year programme target lateral career advancement in these sectors:

**Industries:** IT and tech companies, BFSI, Manufacturing, Consulting firms (HR advisory), Pharmaceuticals, E-commerce, Conglomerates

**Mid-level roles (typical for 1-year MBA candidates):** HR Business Partner, Talent Acquisition Lead, L&D Manager, Total Rewards Manager, Employee Experience Manager, HR Analytics Lead

**Senior roles (5-8+ years experience):** Head of HR, VP People and Culture, CHRO (mid-size firms), Director Talent Management, Head of Total Rewards, Global HR Lead

**Target organisations:** MNCs with India operations, top-tier IT services firms, consulting firms with HR advisory practices, and high-growth startups scaling their people function.

Note: JGU's 1-year MBA is designed for working professionals seeking career acceleration, not entry-level placement. The programme does not offer campus placements.

skills.body:
The JGU HRM specialisation builds skills that senior HR leaders and CHROs test during lateral hiring:

**Technical skills:**
- Performance Management: Design OKR cascades, build calibration processes, implement continuous feedback systems, and link performance data to compensation and succession decisions
- Talent Management: Build strategic workforce plans, create succession pipelines for critical roles, design employer branding strategies, and implement high-potential identification frameworks
- Compensation and Benefits: Conduct market benchmarking, design total rewards structures (base, variable, equity, benefits), perform pay equity audits, and build compensation philosophy documents
- Employee Wellbeing: Design mental health support programmes, build burnout prevention frameworks, implement flexible work policies, and measure wellbeing ROI for business cases

**Strategic skills:**
- HR Analytics: Use people data for attrition prediction, engagement driver analysis, and workforce cost modelling to present data-driven recommendations to leadership
- Organisational Design: Restructure teams and reporting lines to align people strategy with business transformation goals

**Leadership skills:** Lead HR transformation projects, present people strategy to boards, manage cross-functional stakeholders during organisational change, and build HR team capability.

comparisons.body:
JGU is a premium-tier 1-year MBA. Comparisons reflect this positioning.

**vs MAHE Online MBA (Rs 2,92,000, 2-year, NIRF #3):** MAHE offers NIRF #3 ranking and a well-established 2-year format with deep HR specialisation content. JGU offers AACSB accreditation (global gold standard), IoE status, and completion in 12 months instead of 24. For HR professionals who cannot commit to two years alongside demanding HRBP or talent roles, JGU's accelerated format is the key differentiator. MAHE's NIRF ranking carries strong employer recognition in India.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA (Rs 1,96,000-2,20,000, 2-year):** NMIMS carries strong brand recognition in corporate India. JGU offers AACSB accreditation and 1-year completion. For HR professionals targeting MNC roles or international career mobility, JGU's AACSB credential is more portable. NMIMS serves professionals who prefer the 2-year pacing and Mumbai brand equity.

**vs Amity Online MBA (Rs 2,25,000, 2-year, NIRF #22):** Amity offers broad HR elective choices and NIRF #22 at accessible fees. JGU targets a premium segment with AACSB, IoE status, and the 1-year accelerated format. For experienced HR professionals (5+ years) who value credential quality and global recognition, JGU is positioned higher. Amity serves a broader audience including early-career HR professionals.

fees.body:
O.P. Jindal Global University 1-Year Online MBA in Human Resource Management fee structure is available upon application. EMI starting at Rs 6,319 per month directly through the university.

JGU is an Institution of Eminence declared by the Ministry of Education, AACSB accredited (global gold standard held by ~6% of B-schools worldwide), and QS 5 Star rated. The 1-year format (12 months, 72 credits) is enabled by JGU's IoE status under UGC Regulations 2017.

Contact our counsellor for current fee structure, payment options, and EMI tenure. EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with JGU each quarter. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The O.P. Jindal Global University MBA degree certificate carries the JGU name, the MBA designation, and identifies the Human Resource Management specialisation. JGU is UGC recognised, holds Institution of Eminence status from the Ministry of Education, and is AACSB accredited. The AACSB accreditation is recognised globally and signals that JGU's business programmes meet international quality benchmarks. The degree is valid for employment, promotions, higher studies, and international credential evaluation. JGU's IoE status and AACSB accreditation distinguish this certificate from standard online MBA degrees in India.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from JGU 1-Year Online MBA HRM learners. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Priya Menon
items.liked: Talent Management and Employee Wellbeing modules were directly applicable to my HRBP role at a tech company.
items.disliked: Fee information is not listed on the website. Had to speak with admissions to understand the full cost.
items.body: I have been an HRBP at a mid-size tech company for 5 years. The 1-year format was the only reason I pursued an MBA. I could not justify two years off from active workforce planning cycles. The Talent Management module helped me redesign our succession planning framework. Employee Wellbeing gave me data-backed arguments for our mental health programme. The AACSB tag matters when I speak with our global HR leadership. Promoted to Senior HRBP within 3 months of completing the programme.

items.name: Rahul Sharma
items.liked: Performance Management module changed how I design appraisal cycles. Compensation and Benefits gave me benchmarking frameworks I use weekly.
items.disliked: The 12-month intensity is genuinely demanding. Expect 15-20 hours per week if you are working full-time.
items.body: I lead total rewards at a manufacturing conglomerate with 8 years in HR. Wanted an MBA to move into a CHRO-track role. JGU's 1-year format and AACSB accreditation were the deciding factors over 2-year alternatives. The Compensation and Benefits elective was the most practical module I have taken in any educational programme. I built a pay equity audit framework during the coursework that we implemented company-wide. The IoE status gives me confidence this degree will hold value for the next 10-15 years.

items.name: Anjali Gupta
items.liked: Core subjects (Strategy, Analytics, Financial Management) gave me business acumen that HR professionals often lack.
items.disliked: No placement support, which is expected for a working professional programme but should be communicated clearly during admission.
items.body: I was a talent acquisition manager wanting to transition to a broader HR leadership role. The core MBA subjects were as valuable as the HR electives. Financial Management and Business Analytics gave me the business language to sit at leadership tables. The AACSB credential was recognised when I applied for a Head of People role at a Series C startup. Got the offer. The 72 credits in 12 months is intense but structured well enough to manage alongside a full-time role.

faqs.question: How can JGU offer a 1-year MBA?
faqs.answer: JGU holds Institution of Eminence (IoE) status under UGC (Institutions of Eminence Deemed to be Universities) Regulations, 2017. IoE status grants autonomy for innovative programme design, including accelerated formats. The 1-year MBA covers 72 credits, the same credit load as conventional 2-year MBA programmes, compressed into 12 months. Only four private universities in India hold IoE status.

faqs.question: What is AACSB accreditation?
faqs.answer: AACSB (Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business) is the global gold standard for business school accreditation. Only about 6% of business schools worldwide hold AACSB accreditation, and roughly 1% in India. It signals that the institution meets rigorous international benchmarks for curriculum, faculty qualifications, and learning outcomes. AACSB-accredited degrees are recognised by global employers and universities for further studies.

faqs.question: Is JGU's 1-year MBA valid?
faqs.answer: Yes. JGU is UGC recognised and holds IoE status from the Ministry of Education. The programme is AACSB accredited and covers 72 credits, meeting UGC norms. The degree is valid for employment, promotions, and higher studies in India and internationally.

faqs.question: Can I pursue SHRM or CIPD certification alongside this MBA?
faqs.answer: Yes. The HRM specialisation content (performance management, compensation, talent management) aligns well with SHRM-CP/SCP and CIPD frameworks. The 1-year MBA format means you complete the degree first and can then pursue SHRM or CIPD certification without overlapping commitments. The AACSB credential combined with SHRM or CIPD creates a strong HR leadership profile.

faqs.question: Is this MBA suitable for transitioning into HR from another function?
faqs.answer: The programme is designed for professionals with existing work experience. If you have 3+ years in any function and want to transition into HR leadership, the core subjects provide management fundamentals while the HRM electives build specialised people management skills. The Organisational Behaviour core subject and the four HRM electives together cover the knowledge base expected for HRBP and HR manager roles.

chooseIf: You are an HR professional with 3-8 years of experience who wants an MBA completed in 12 months, not 24, without compromising on credential quality.
You value AACSB accreditation and IoE status for global HR roles at MNCs or international career mobility.
You want a premium MBA credential that gives you business acumen alongside HR specialisation to move into CHRO-track positions.
consider: You are early in your career (0-2 years) and need campus placement support. JGU's 1-year MBA is designed for working professionals, not entry-level hiring pipelines.
You want the lowest possible fee for an HR MBA. JGU is premium-priced. Consider IGNOU or LPU for budget-optimised options.
You want deep labour law and industrial relations coverage. JGU's HRM electives focus on talent management, performance, compensation, and wellbeing rather than legal and compliance tracks.
